name: Mining Basins Route
name_original: Salinas de Pisuerga, Cervera de Pisuerga y Villabellaco – circular desde Barruelo de Santullán
description: This difficult 32.2-mile touring cycling route from Barruelo de Santullán explores the Montaña Palentina, featuring villages and mountain vi
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Barruelo De Santullán traverse the diverse landscapes of the Montaña Palentina in northern Spain. The region features a mix of gentle plains and challenging mountain passes, offering varied terrain for cyclists. Key natural elements include the Rubagón River, ancient oak groves, and the dramatic Sierra de Híjar. This area is characterized by its rich mining heritage and numerous Romanesque art sites, providing a unique backdrop for cycling adventures. Best touring cycling routes around Barruelo De Santullán The most popular touring cycling route is Mining Basins Route, a 32.2 miles (51.9 km) trail that takes 3 hours 45 minutes to complete. This route explores the historical mining areas of Castilla y León. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Vallespinoso de Aguilar – circular desde Salinas de Pisuerga, a difficult 16.9 miles (27.2 km) path. This trail offers views of the Montaña Palentina and passes through charming villages. Local touring cyclists also love the Hermitage of Santa Eulalia – Aguilar Reservoir loop from Salinas de Pisuerga, a 28.9 miles (46.6 km) trail leading through the scenic Aguilar Reservoir area, often completed in about 3 hours 45 minutes. Touring cycling around Barruelo De Santullán is defined by its Montaña Palentina landscapes, historical mining heritage, and Romanesque art. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy rides to challenging mountain passes. The routes in Barruelo De Santullán are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 300 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Barruelo De Santullán's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Barruelo de Santullán?
A: Barruelo de Santullán offers a wide selection of touring cycling routes, with over 50 options available. These routes cater to various skill levels, from easy rides to more challenging mountain passes, ensuring there's something for every cyclist.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for families or beginners?
A: Yes, Barruelo de Santullán has 5 easy touring cycling routes, perfect for families or those new to touring cycling. The region's diverse terrain includes gentle plains, making it accessible for all fitness levels. While specific family-friendly routes are not detailed, the easier options provide a good starting point.

Q: Can I find circular touring cycling routes in the Barruelo de Santullán area?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in Barruelo de Santullán are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1174349817/vallespinoso-de-aguilar-circular-desde-salinas-de-pisuerga" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vallespinoso de Aguilar – circular desde Salinas de Pisuerga&lt;/a&gt; route is a popular 27.2 km loop that offers views of the Montaña Palentina and passes through charming villages. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38175442" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Santa Eulalia – Aguilar Reservoir loop from Salinas de Pisuerga&lt;/a&gt;, a scenic 46.6 km trail.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes around Barruelo de Santullán?
A: The terrain around Barruelo de Santullán is highly diverse, ranging from gentle plains to challenging mountain passes within the Montaña Palentina. You'll encounter well-preserved and lightly traveled roads and paths, suitable for road, gravel, and mountain biking. The region's geology also features distinctive formations like 'Las Tuerces' and the dramatic Horadada Canyon, adding to the varied landscape.

Q: What natural features or viewpoints can I see while cycling in the region?
A: Cycling in Barruelo de Santullán offers numerous natural highlights. You can explore the dramatic landscapes of the Montaña Palentina and the impressive Sierra de Híjar, which offers panoramic views. The La Pedrosa Natural Area, traversed by the Rubagón River, features picturesque waterfalls and pools like Los Pilones and El Calizón. You might also encounter ancient oak groves, beeches, and birches. For a significant natural landmark, consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3837834" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pisuerga River Source (Cueva del Cobre)&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural attractions along the touring cycling routes?
A: Yes, the region is rich in history and culture. Barruelo de Santullán's identity is deeply rooted in its coal mining past, with routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1170948303/mining-basins-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mining Basins Route&lt;/a&gt; exploring historical mining areas. The area is also a cradle of Romanesque art, featuring well-preserved monuments and churches. You can visit the Romanesque Iglesia de Santo Tomás in Barruelo, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4848732" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa María la Real Monastery&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3306399" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Santa Cecilia&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Barruelo de Santullán?
A: The Montaña Palentina region is particularly vibrant in autumn, with mosses, lichens, and heather creating a colorful landscape. Spring and early summer also offer pleasant conditions for cycling, with milder temperatures and blooming flora. While cycling is possible year-round, checking local weather forecasts is always recommended, especially for mountain passes.

Q: Where can I park my car when starting a touring cycling route in Barruelo de Santullán?
A: Barruelo de Santullán and its surrounding villages, which often serve as starting points for routes, typically offer parking facilities. For routes starting from Cillamayor, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24633807" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aguilar Reservoir – Collegiate Church of St. Michael loop&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31879970" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto del Bardal – Historic Bridge of Reinosilla loop&lt;/a&gt;, you can usually find parking within the village.

Q: What do other cyclists enjoy most about touring cycling in Barruelo de Santullán?
A: The touring cycling routes in Barruelo de Santullán are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 30 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, the rich historical and cultural elements like the mining heritage and Romanesque art, and the variety of routes suitable for different skill levels. The lightly traveled roads and scenic paths are also frequently highlighted.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter while cycling in the Montaña Palentina?
A: The Montaña Palentina is home to diverse wildlife. While cycling, you might spot wild species such as roe deer and wildcats. The area is also a haven for birds, including golden eagles and griffon vultures. The Rubagón River, which flows through the region, is known for its common trout. Keep an eye out for these creatures, especially in quieter, more natural sections of the routes.

Q: Are there any touring cycling routes that pass through charming villages?
A: Absolutely. Many routes in the Barruelo de Santullán area are designed to take you through picturesque villages, offering a glimpse into local culture and architecture. Villages like Porquera de Santullán, Revilla de Santullán, Cillamayor, Santa María de Nava, Villabellaco, and Brañosera are often part of the cycling network, providing opportunities to experience the authentic character of the region.
tips: This track is fun, calm and very rolling. It consists of small ups and downs of little unevenness.

Salinas de Pisuerga is a small town where the medieval bridge that crosses the waters of the Pisuerga River deserves special attention. It was built in the 16th century and was converted into the main access road to the municipality.
Nice railway bridge that crosses the Pisuerga river on the outskirts of Salinas de Pisuerga.

Cervera de Pisuerga is a small tourist mountain town in the heart of the Palencia Mountain, part of the Cantabrian Mountains. The landscape and views will blow you away. An amazing place to bike and explore.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this touring cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as 'difficult' and a 'Hard bike ride', requiring a very good fitness level. It's not recommended for beginners due to its demanding nature, varied slopes, and sections that may require dismounting and pushing your bike.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the route?
faqs.answer: The route features a mix of paved and unpaved sections. While much of it is paved, be prepared for difficult terrain, including compacted dirt tracks, rural trails, and potentially rocky or loose gravel sections, especially if you're on a gravel bike. Some parts may necessitate dismounting and pushing your bike.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the 'Salinas de Pisuerga, Cervera de Pisuerga y Villabellaco – circular desde Barruelo de Santullán' is a circular route, meaning you will finish at your starting point in Barruelo de Santullán.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail in Barruelo de Santullán?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas are not detailed, Barruelo de Santullán serves as the starting and ending point for this circular route. You should be able to find suitable parking within the village to begin your ride.

faqs.question: What are the best times of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Montaña Palentina region is best enjoyed during spring, summer, and early autumn. These seasons offer more favorable weather conditions for cycling, allowing you to fully appreciate the stunning landscapes and avoid potential snow or harsh winter conditions in the Cantabrian Mountains.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: The route offers diverse scenery and historical sites. You'll pass through picturesque villages, encounter Romanesque architecture, and cycle alongside the Pisuerga River. Notable highlights include the Salinas de Pisuerga with its 16th-century church and medieval bridge, the Cervera de Pisuerga area known for its panoramic views, and the Pista de Grilleras. You'll also cross the Railway Bridge over the Pisuerga River.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of several other trails, including the Ruta de las Cuencas Mineras del Carbón, Camino Olvidado (Matamorisca-Puente Almuhey), and the Camino Natural del Románico Palentino. These intersections highlight the region's natural beauty and historical significance, particularly its mining heritage and Romanesque art.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this route?
faqs.answer: The route passes through the Montaña Palentina Natural Park. Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for cycling on public trails within the park. However, always respect local regulations, especially concerning protected areas and private land.

faqs.question: Is the Salinas de Pisuerga, Cervera de Pisuerga y Villabellaco route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally allowed on many trails in the Montaña Palentina Natural Park, it's crucial to keep them on a leash, especially given the presence of wildlife and agricultural areas. Due to the challenging nature of this specific touring bicycle route, consider if it's suitable for your dog's fitness and comfort over such a distance and varied terrain.

faqs.question: What kind of views and natural landscapes can I expect?
faqs.answer: The route offers stunning and diverse landscapes within the Montaña Palentina Natural Park. You'll experience panoramic views of the Cantabrian Mountains, including potential snow-capped peaks, deep valleys, and beautiful beech forests. The journey also includes cycling alongside the Pisuerga River and through charming villages.

faqs.question: Are there any places to eat or drink along the route?
faqs.answer: The route passes through several villages such as Salinas de Pisuerga and Cervera de Pisuerga. These settlements typically offer options for refreshments, including cafes, restaurants, or small shops where you can refuel during your ride.

waypoints.name: Railway Bridge over the Pisuerga River
